response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily. This means David collected 39 blueberries. In addition David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia. Therefore Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected 56 blueberries in total. 

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. They made 6 batches of muffins. Each batch was sold for $5. Therefore they made 30 dollars from the muffins. The money they made came from the blueberries they collected. 

***

The process of making muffins involved measuring and mixing the blueberries. They used all the blueberries they had collected. The muffins were baked and then sold to customers. The customers paid for the muffins and the money went to the group. 

***

The money they made from the muffins was 30 dollars. This amount reflected the value of the blueberries they collected. The blueberries were gathered by Emily David and Sophia. The youth who collected the blueberries made money through their efforts.
